entries.summary: Deploy, failover, diagnostics, release-verification, and public-surface truth for the Mesh launch path were already shipped in M053 and M054 even though multiple mesh-lang tracker rows remain open.
entries.delivery_truth: shipped across M053 and M054; the tracker rows are stale shipped-but-open language work
entries.workspace_path_truth: mesh-lang compiler/scripts/docs surfaces remain tracked directly in this repo
entries.proposed_tracker_action: Close or rewrite the shipped-but-open tracker rows so they point at the delivered M053/M054 contract instead of describing those launch foundations as pending work.

evidence_refs.marker: M053 started by making the generated Postgres Todo starter own a real staged deploy handoff
evidence_refs.note: M053 summary records the staged deploy bundle and serious deploy contract as shipped.

evidence_refs.marker: the generated Postgres starter is the serious deployable/shared path
evidence_refs.note: M053 summary records docs/public-surface truth for the serious deployable starter.

evidence_refs.marker: one public app URL may choose ingress
evidence_refs.note: M054 summary records the bounded one-public-URL runtime contract as shipped.
M054 summary records shipped direct request-key follow-through and diagnostics truth.
Representative shipped-but-open mesh-lang issue in the deploy/runtime family.

entries.summary: frontend-exp exists, but the checked-in operator UI is still backed by MOCK_ISSUES and MOCK_STATS, so the app/backend replacement work remains partially delivered rather than shipped.
entries.delivery_truth: partial only; real backend/data workflows for the operator app remain open
entries.workspace_path_truth: mesher/frontend-exp/lib/mock-data.ts via the compatibility symlink into the sibling product repo
entries.proposed_tracker_action: Keep the product app/backend issues open, and describe them as mock-backed frontend-exp follow-through instead of implying the real operator app already shipped.

evidence_refs.note: frontend-exp still exports hard-coded issue data.
frontend-exp still exports hard-coded dashboard stats.

evidence_refs.marker: the sibling product repo (`hyperpush-org/hyperpush`, still surfaced locally through the `hyperpush-mono` workspace path) owns `mesher/`, `mesher/landing/`, and `mesher/frontend-exp/`
evidence_refs.note: Project contract names frontend-exp as a product-owned surface.

entries.summary: hyperpush#8 is a real website/docs bug, but the issue body points only at mesh-lang-owned VitePress files, so the tracker row belongs in mesh-lang rather than the product repo.
entries.delivery_truth: active docs bug on mesh-lang-owned files; current repo placement is wrong
entries.proposed_tracker_action: Move or recreate hyperpush#8 under mesh-lang and relabel it as a language-repo docs/packages-nav bug.

evidence_refs.note: Issue body explicitly cites mesh-lang docs files and asks for packages.meshlang.dev routing.
The nav config cited by hyperpush#8 is a mesh-lang docs file.
The custom NavBar cited by hyperpush#8 is also a mesh-lang docs file.

entries.summary: The evaluator-facing /pitch route already shipped in the sibling product repo during M056, but there is still no dedicated repo issue or project row that records that delivered surface.
entries.delivery_truth: shipped in M056 without dedicated tracker coverage
entries.workspace_path_truth: mesher/landing/app/pitch/page.tsx via compatibility symlink into the sibling product repo
entries.proposed_tracker_action: Create or repoint one hyperpush tracker row so the shipped /pitch route is represented explicitly instead of being implied only by milestone history.

evidence_refs.note: The shipped /pitch route still exists in the product surface.

evidence_refs.marker: The evaluator-facing `/pitch` route remains the maintained landing artifact there
evidence_refs.note: Project contract says /pitch lives in the sibling product repo after the split.

entries.summary: Tracker wording still has to normalize stale hyperpush-mono references to the public hyperpush repo identity whenever a product-owned row points at mesher, landing, or frontend-exp work.
entries.ownership_truth: product-owned in the Hyperpush product repo
entries.delivery_truth: naming normalization remains active reconciliation work, even though the repo split itself already shipped in M055
entries.workspace_path_truth: local compatibility path mesher -> ../hyperpush-mono/mesher; workspace helper canonicalizes both hyperpush and hyperpush-mono to workspace root hyperpush-mono
entries.proposed_tracker_action: Rewrite stale hyperpush-mono repo mentions on repo issues and project items to the canonical public hyperpush destination while preserving the local hyperpush-mono workspace path only as implementation detail.

evidence_refs.marker: still surfaced locally through the `hyperpush-mono` workspace path
evidence_refs.note: Project contract distinguishes the local workspace alias from the public product repo identity.
workspace-git already accepts the public hyperpush remote as the canonical repo identity.
Live GitHub inventory capture canonicalized hyperpush-mono to hyperpush.

evidence_refs.marker: M055 delivered the repo-boundary split as a contract-and-proof milestone
evidence_refs.note: M055 summary records the split and naming/ownership reset as shipped.

decision_refs.note: D454 defines the three-layer artifact split for raw snapshots, derived evidence, and the final ledger.
D455 defines the canonical issue URL inventory shape that this evidence index consumes.
D456 fixes the downstream naming contract by keeping workspace-path truth separate from public repo truth and canonical tracker destination.
